mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.5 percentage points within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in real GDP growth indicates a supply-demand mismatch that erodes corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or the combined Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 325, an increase in the federal funds rate accelerates discount rate growth faster than earnings can adjust, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when both CPI exceeds 325 and FEDFUNDS rises, or the signal's predictive power vanishes in low-inflation periods (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenue expectations, which mechanically supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns following payroll growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When monthly housing starts fall below 1.2 million units, it signals a sharp deceleration in residential investment and consumer confidence, which transmits to broader equity markets and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the housing starts decline, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate combined with a federal funds rate above 3.5 percent creates a double squeeze on household disposable income and borrowing costs, severely compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or the combined Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid industrial capacity expansion where INDPRO rises above 105, a subsequent decline in commercial paper indicates credit tightening that restricts working capital for manufacturers, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when both industrial production exceeds 105 and commercial paper declines, or the signal's predictive power vanishes in low-growth periods (Rank IC < 0.01).
